phase2.errorMessage:
(Program runs forever! No error message — but the code never stops.)

Sum: 1
Sum: 2
Sum: 3
Sum: 4
Sum: 5
Sum: 6
Sum: 7
... infinite ...
phase2.explanation:
This is an infinite loop! counter never changes, so counter <= 5 is ALWAYS true.

The fix: counter++ inside the loop so it eventually becomes 6 and the condition becomes false.

Always check: does your loop variable change? Is there a path to exit?

Tips to avoid infinite loops:
• Make sure your condition will become false
• Use break as an escape hatch
• Print debugging messages to track variables

phase3.prompt:
Write a program that:
1. Uses a for loop to print numbers 10 down to 1
2. Uses a while loop to add numbers 1 to 100 and print the sum (5050)
dialogueHints: For the for loop: start at 10, run while >= 1, decrement each time
For the while loop: start sum=0, num=1, add until num=100
Print the final sum to verify it's 5050
phase3.openingDialogue: Time to loop — don't get caught in an infinite one! 🔄
